name: Traffic Incidents
description: This is an unofficial archive of traffic incidents within Calgary. Traffic incidents are traffic disruptions affecting traffic flow, such as traffic signal issues, hazardous road conditions, stalled vehicles, and unverified, unreported traffic collisions. This data is based off unrecorded imagery where traffic cameras are present. There may be gaps in the data due to system or script malfunction. For more information, see the Calgary Traffic Report page.

columns.name: JOBSTATUSDESC
columns.description: •Pending Recommendation - The minimum requirements for that specific licence have not all been met so the Licence status is pending. •Pending Renewal - 60 days prior to expiry •Renewal Invoiced - 45 days prior to the Business Licence expiring, a renewal invoice is generated and mailed out.  The status of the Business Licence will read as Pending Renewal/Renewal Invoiced until the invoice is paid or the expiry of the Licence. •Expired - The licence has lapsed and the renewal has not been paid  •Invoiced - The business has met all licensing requirements and an invoice has been mailed. The business is not licensed until the invoice is paid. •Licensed - All requirements have been met, the invoice has been paid and this new business is currently licensed. •Renewal Licensed - This is a renewal licence that has been paid.

columns.name: GETBUSID
columns.description: Business Licence ID

description: This file includes the licensed businesses in the City of Calgary. Data fields include: Trade name, Business Location, Creation Date, Status, Licence Type and Community District of the business. All businesses created before October 7, 1994 reflect this date as their anniversary date. Not all business require a municipal licence but may still require licensing, permits or inspections from other regulatory bodies. For more information on business licensing, visit: Manage your business

name: Traffic Cameras
description: View up-to-the-minute traffic camera images of current road conditions on major routes as well as other key intersections. *These cameras are solely for traffic monitoring and not enforcement. Camera footage is not recorded. Cameras can be down for maintenance or in the case of collisions, to protect the identity of those involved.*

name: Community Crime Statistics
description: As of 2024, data is no longer provided by the Calgary Police Service. To access latest data click here. This data is considered cumulative as late-reported incidents are often received well after an offence has occurred. Therefore, crime counts are subject to change as they are updated. Crime count is based on the most serious violation (MSV) per incident. Violence: These figures include all violent crime offences as defined by the Centre for Canadian Justice Statistics Universal Crime Reporting

name: Calgary Transit Routes

columns.name: ROUTE_CATEGORY
columns.description: The category of the route. “LRT” refers to light rail transit, i.e. the CTrain. “BRT” refers to bus rapid transit, i.e. routes designed to transport passengers over long distances quickly, with few stops. “SCHOOL” refers to routes designed to serve students attending school. “REGULAR” refers to typical Calgary Transit bus routes. “SPECIAL” refers to routes providing specialized service that do not fall into the previous categories.

description: This is a map layer representing the routes in the Calgary Transit Scheduling dataset. For more information on the General Transit Feed Specification click here. A dataset of Calgary Transit Stops is also available for download.

columns.name: TREE_MAINTENANCE
columns.description: The current general maintenance level provided to the tree based up tree stage of life. ESTABLISH TREE – A young tree that needs help to get established and generally receives watering and higher maintenance; MAINTAIN TREE – An established, mature tree that only receives maintenance as needed; MAINTAIN SHRUB – Shrub that will only receive maintenance as needed; PLANT SITE – After a tree and its stump are removed, this indicates it may be a good spot to plant a new tree; STUMP REMOVAL – The tree has been removed, and a stump remains which requires removal.

columns.name: HOUSE_ALPHA
columns.description: An alphabetic extension added to an address. (1"A" Street SW)

columns.name: STREET_QUAD
columns.description: The city of Calgary is divided into four quadrants. The Bow River and the main north-south bridge (Centre Street Bridge) are the central axis with Centre Street dividing east from west and the Bow River dividing north from south, resulting in four quadrants (SW, SE, NE, NW). With expansion of Calgary, the quadrant definitions have changed to accommodate natural physical features (Bow River) and manmade facilities (Macleod Trail).

columns.name: HOUSE_NUMBER
columns.description:
All properties along a roadway are assigned numbers consecutively, usually starting at one end of the roadway and terminating at the opposite end, to represent an incremental distance along the roadway. 
In a house number on a numbered roadway, the last two digits are graduating numbers which increase as one progresses down a roadway. The leading numbers, 0-3 digits, represent the hundreds block. A house number on a named roadway may also be determined by the grid system or by an arbitrary starting point on that roadway.

name: Public Water Service Lines
description: A water service pipe, also known as a water service line, is the way drinking water is delivered to each home in Calgary. The water service pipe can be thought of as two sections: (1) From the water main to the property line: This part of the pipe is owned and maintained by The City of Calgary. (2) From the property line to your house: This part of the pipe is owned and maintained by the homeowner. The program monitors surface water quality across a network of sites that include rivers, tributaries, and reservoirs within the Calgary region. description: These 3D building shapes are derived from full surface LiDAR by creating roof surface outlines. Exterior walls are projected from the outer edges of the roof outlines to just below the ground surface. The open dataset is not strictly 3D. To download 3D buildings data in Multipatch GDB format (0.35 GB), click zipped geodatabase attachment file, or for 3D buildings in CAD file format (1.17 GB), click zipped Autocad file. The projection is NAD83 Alberta 3TM 114W (EPSG:3776).

It is an analysis of residential rooftops (excluding apartment complexes) to estimate a potential capacity of a PV installation. The analysis uses LiDAR data taken in 2022 to estimate solar irradiance and roof shape to estimate the number of 1m x 2m solar panels. columns.name: INT_TYPE
columns.description: This field identifies the type of traffic signal•Traffic signal: Signalized system that manages traffic flow at an intersection.•Traffic signal T intersection: Traffic signal installed at a T intersection.•1/2 signal: Traffic signal that manages 2 directions of traffic flow.•1/4 signal:  Traffic signal that manages one full direction of traffic flow and one left turn movement in the opposite direction.•Fire signal: Traffic signal that is activated by emergency services.•Overhead Flasher: Pedestrian activated crossing system consisting of overhead and/or side mounted flashing amber beacons.•Pedestrian RRFB: Pedestrian Rectangular Rapid Flashing Beacon (RRFB) is a pedestrian activated crossing system consisting of side mounted flashing beacons that flash in an irregular manner.

name: Bike and Pedestrian Counts
description: Daily bicycle and pedestrian counts are available from sensors set up on the Peace Bridge, cycle tracks and Stephen Avenue. Using an Eco-Counter machine, cyclists are detected using loops embedded in the pavement. The data provides a breakdown of the number of cyclists on a daily, weekly, monthly and yearly basis.

name: City of Calgary Careers
description: This dataset contains the job opportunities with The City of Calgary. City of Calgary employees operate the facilities, deliver the services and run the programs that support our city. We are committed to building and maintaining a respectful, inclusive and equitable workplace that is representative of the community we serve. We value individuals who bring diverse experience, skills and opinions to our services. We seek individuals who are committed to public service, enjoy collaborating with ot

name: Fair Entry Subsidy Assistance Management System

columns.name: Calgarians Pending
columns.description: Total number of Calgarians assigned a pending status whose application cannot be fully processed until Fair Entry staff receive missing eligibility documents. For example, the applicant is missing a valid proof of income and/or current proof of Calgary residency document, their application is placed in a pending status. Client applications in a pending status for more than 90 days move to a denied status automatically if they have not provided missing documents within 90 days.

columns.name: Calgarians Approved
columns.description: Total number of Calgarians assigned a Fair Entry approved status based on their income and Calgary residency status.

columns.name: Calgarians Applied
columns.description: Total number of persons residing in Calgary who applied to Fair Entry. Note: Calgarians may be in different approval status categories (e.g. denied then moved to approved) within a month and accounted for within each approval status category. As a result, the total number of Calgarians applied will be less than the total number when adding Calgarians approved, denied and pending.

columns.name: Calgarians Denied
columns.description: Total number of Calgarians assigned a denied status are deemed ineligible for Fair Entry due to household income over the federal low-income cut-off threshold when using a Notice of Assessment and/or live outside of Calgary city limits. Denied also includes applications in a pending status over 90 days.

description: Fair Entry is an application process for City subsidized programs and services. This process assesses an applicant’s income eligibility and Calgary residency in order for low-income Calgarians to access multiple City programs with one application. The Fair Entry dataset uses data from the Subsidy Assistance Management System (SAMS), a centralized database that stores personal information of Calgarians applying to The City of Calgary's subsidy programs and services. For more information, please v

description: These are scheduled road closures due to construction, planned maintenance and utility work. Construction detours within The City of Calgary are updated twice a day. (3 am and 3 pm) See Source URL for RSS feed which updates more regularly.

name: Calgary Emergency Shelters Daily Occupancy

columns.name: Capacity
columns.description: The maximum amount of occupants that each shelter is licensed to have residing in the shelter at any given time according to fire code regulations.

columns.name: Daytime
columns.description: The total number of individuals who were served within warming centres or 24/7 shelters in a 24 hour period (12:00am - 12:00am).

columns.name: Overnight
columns.description: The total number of individuals that were admitted to the shelter for an overnight stay at any point in the night.

description: This is a dataset of Calgary area emergency shelter occupancy. It is sourced from the provincial open data source provided by Community and Social Services, and content is limited to Calgary-area shelters. Please visit Emergency Shelters Daily Occupancy AB for more information or other provincial locations.

name: Communities by Ward
description: A list of communities with what Ward they are in. Note: Ward boundaries do not follow community boundaries, therefore there may be some instances where a portion of a community is in an adjacent Ward. Please see the Community and Ward maps to confirm

name: Annual Waste Volumes

columns.name: Household Hazardous Waste Managed (tonnes)
columns.description: Household Hazardous Waste Managed monitors the amount (in tonnes) of material collected from Calgarians through the Household Hazardous Waste (HHW) drop-off program, offered in partnership with the Calgary Fire Department. All year round, residents can take household hazardous materials to the Throw ‘n’ Go areas at The City’s waste management facilities or designated fire stations at no charge.  This program helps to divert chemicals and other hazardous materials from landfills.  All locations accept items such as surplus paint, car batteries, propane tanks, antifreeze, used oil and filters, garden chemicals, aerosols and miscellaneous household products.

columns.name: Waste Disposed at City of Calgary Landfills (tonnes)
columns.description: Waste Disposed at City of Calgary Landfills (tonnes) monitors the total amount of garbage disposed of at the waste management facilities managed by The City of Calgary, including waste from private haulers. This measure does not capture waste that is generated in Calgary and disposed of at facilities not managed by The City of Calgary.

columns.name: Green Cart (tonnes)
columns.description: Green Cart (tonnes) monitors the number of tonnes of material managed in the residential Green Cart (food and yard waste) program per year.  The amount shown for the Green Cart program is the amount of material excluding residue from compost processing, which may be different from the amount collected from customers' Green Carts.   The Green Cart Program was implemented in the second half of 2017, and was completed in conjunction with a change from weekly to every other week Black Cart collection.

columns.name: Blue Cart (tonnes)
columns.description: Blue Cart (tonnes) monitors the number of tonnes of material managed in the residential Blue Cart (recyclables) program per year.  The amount shown for the Blue Cart program is the amount of material shipped or marketed, which may be different from the amount collected from customers' Blue Carts.

columns.name: Black Cart (tonnes)
columns.description: Black Cart (tonnes) monitors the number of tonnes of material managed in the residential Black Cart (garbage) program per year.

description: Annual Waste Volumes provides the number of tonnes of material managed at City of Calgary landfills, through the residential Black (garbage), Blue (recyclables), and Green (food and yard waste) Cart programs, and Household Hazardous Waste program.

description: As part of The City of Calgary's commitment to an open, transparent and accountable local government we release the Senior Management Expense report twice per year in the spring and fall. It includes line item details, including business travel, meals and hosting, and employee recognition for the Senior Management Team. The Senior Management Team includes the City Manager, general managers and directors. Budgets for the City Manager and each General Manager are reviewed and approved annually by

name: Regulatory Flood Map (Bylaw Flood Hazard)
description: Calgary’s Regulatory Flood Map shows the floodway, flood fringe and overland flood zones. The map is used for land planning purposes, showing the designated flood zones where various development and building regulations apply. The map shows the regulatory flood zones for the Bow River, Elbow River, Nose Creek and West Nose Creek. An annotation file is provided separately as an attachment: (Bylaw Flood Hazard Annotations). This map is the 1:100 flood extent calculated in 1983. Designated flood el

name: Alberta Township Sections
description: The Alberta Township File identifies land (section, township, range) in a grid format known as Dominion Land Survey (DLS) system. It is a graphic representation of these section boundaries (approximately 1 mile X 1 mile). The file contains Section Boundary, Section Name, Original Road Allowance and Quarter Section attribute information. The polygons in this file represent the section boundaries determined in 1997 by examination and review of all registered legal plans in The City of Calgary. Eac

columns.description: Count of inactive dwelling units. A dwelling unit that is part of a multi-dwelling unit structure and is being used as part of another dwelling unit in the same physical structure (e.g. both halves of a duplex used by one family); or a vacant pad in a mobile home park; or a converted structure unit like a basement suite, which is not occupied as a dwelling unit, has not been removed, is not being renovated but is not available for occupancy; or a dwelling unit that has been boarded-up, scheduled for demolition, fenced or taped off, condemned, or otherwise uninhabitable.

name: Wastewater Treatment Plants Effluent Water Quality
description: This dataset contains results from the City of Calgary's Wastewater Treatment Plants that are reported monthly to Alberta Environment and Protected Areas. Results include laboratory and flow data for the wastewater treatment plants’ treated effluents, 2007 to present. Sampling locations have been added, removed, or modified over time. All laboratory results have been determined from the daily 24-hour composite samples, with the exception of Fecal Coliforms which are determined from the grab samp

name: Hydrology
description: An extent of land covered by, intermittent and permanent, water bodies and watercourses. This includes both parcel bounding and non-parcel bounding occurrences. Includes the natural watercourses as follows: Bow River Elbow River Nose Creek West Nose Creek Fish Creek. This is a generalized version of the data set and is intended for cartographic purposes and faster drawing time.

name: Non permanent streams
description: This digital data set identifies, maps, and classifies ephemeral and intermittent streams in Calgary. This data set is intended for mapping at scales between 1:5,000 and 1:50,000, for the purpose of higher-level analysis and planning, or as a starting point to locate areas of interest to focus more detailed field work and surveys. Please note that the data is not intended to replace or supercede existing requirements during the process of planning, subdivision, and development, including field i

name: Traffic Volumes for 2023
description: Average Daily Weekday Traffic (ADWT) is the average number of vehicles in 24 hours adjusted for seasonal variation to represent the average weekday. The number on this map represents two-way totals. Volume on Provincial roads are provided by Alberta Transportation as a Weighted Average Annual Daily Traffic(WAADT). This traffic flow map is intended to be a general representation of traffic volumes across the city. Traffic volumes shown are based on 2023 data and may reflect prevailing conditions

name: Calgary Equity Index Matrix

columns.name: COLOUR
columns.description: The colour assigned to a CSA based on the status or score. Each colour communicates a different equity concern. Green areas are at or above the target cut-off and describes areas with a score that signal the least overall equity concern. The red areas are at or below the benchmark cut-off and describes areas with a score that signals high overall burden of inequities. Finally, yellow areas fall between the green and red areas

columns.name: QUADRANT
columns.description: The four boundary parts into which Calgary is divided into. These are the NE North East, SE South East, NE North West, and SW South West

columns.description: Provides information on the year or date data was procured

columns.name: DOMAIN
columns.description: The themes or categories of data types that is used to measure the Calgary Equity Index. Each domain is made of indicator variables

columns.name: STATUS
columns.description: The designation of a CSA score relative to the target cut-off range for the indicator variable.  Based on the score, the CSAs are designated either as above target, below benchmark or between target and benchmark. The status assigned to a CSA defines the equity concern for the CSA

description: The Calgary Equity Index is a decision-making tool designed to measure equity in Calgary, based on a social determinant of health (SDOH) framework. The SDOH are the range of interacting social and economic conditions that influence people’s health and well-being. This index provides an equity lens to examine the ways in which social and economic conditions are experienced and distributed among populations. It will help the City examine where inequities exist in different areas. Information is av

It does not list the default residential speed limit of 40 km/hr, which was reduced from 50 km/h to 40 km/h effective May 31, 2021. This dataset is used for identification and is not considered an official record of speed limits for any given time. It is not used for enforcement purposes.

name: Water Quality Monitoring Sonde Data

columns.name: Field Data Description
columns.description: Continuous: In situ measurements logged at regular intervals (e.g. 15 minutes) for an extended period of time. Discrete: Individual in situ measurements manually collected by a field technician.

description: This dataset is compiled from The City of Calgary’s in situ (in location) water quality monitoring program. In situ field measurements are acquired from the deployment of multi-parameter sondes in surface waters by The City of Calgary. Measurements are collected using two methods: Continuous and Discrete. Continuous: In situ measurements logged at regular intervals (e.g. 15 minutes) for an extended period of time. Discrete: Individual in situ measurements manually collected by a field technician

description: Information about artworks in The City of Calgary Public Art Collection, including locations, titles, artists, and descriptions of the artwork. For more information, visit: www.calgary.ca/publicart

name: Assessment Review Board Complaints

columns.name: Community
columns.description: The name given to the district of the situated parcel. Calgary is divided into areas as they are subdivided/developed, and each area is known as a community

columns.name: Board's Decision
columns.description: The outcome from the complaint filed. •	Decrease – The board issued a decrease in the assessment amount. •	No change – There was no change to the assessment. •	Resolved without Hearing – There was no hearing that took place.

columns.name: Assessment Value
columns.description: Indicates the total assessment value for the property or portion of the property in the event there is any related value

columns.name: After Value
columns.description: Indicates the after value for the property or portion of the property from the event of a hearing that took place

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The year prior to the taxation year

columns.name: Property Use
columns.description: The categorization of the property for assessment purposes. The property uses are: agricultural, commercial, environmental reserve, industrial, institutional, linear property, multi-residential, municipal reserve, municipal school reserve, recreational, single residential and transportation.

columns.name: File Number
columns.description: The unique identifier for the roll number under compliant for the year the complaint was filed

columns.name: Location Address
columns.description: The municipally assigned address for each property

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The outcome of the complaint. •Assessment Amendment – Assessment Business Unit corrected the Assessment. •	Hearing Occurred – There was a decision as a result of a hearing. •	Mutual agreement – Both parties agreed mutually on an outcome which canceled the hearing. • Withdrawn – The complainant withdrew their compliant.

columns.name: Hearing Level
columns.description: CARB - Composite Assessment Review Board; LARB - Local Assessment Board

description: This dataset contains decisions, mutual agreements, withdrawals and assessment amendments. For more information on the Calgary Assessment Review Board (ARB) please visit: Calgary Assessment Review Board. You can also explore the Evidence Disclosure Portal. See attached: data dictionary.

name: City of Calgary Events
description: Public meetings, engagement, recreation, and other events around the City. This file is only updated twice daily: in the morning and afternoon. For instant RSS notification, see: https://www.trumba.com/calendars/map-feeds.rss

name: Recreation Program Listings
description: City of Calgary Registered Programs. Each program can have multiple sessions (classes) and therefore each class is listed in a new row. See Live and Play for more information.

name: Calgary Transit Stops By Route Cross Reference

columns.name: ROUTE_SHORT_NAME
columns.description: The number identifying the route, i.e. its short name

columns.name: TELERIDE_NUMBER
columns.description: A unique identifier for the stop that is also displayed on a sign at the stop location and may be used to access schedule information.

columns.name: STOP_NAME
columns.description: The name of the stop, typically containing the direction of travel, the street the stop is located on, and a nearby cross-street.

columns.name: STATUS
columns.description: Indicates whether the stop is in use (ACTIVE) or not (INACTIVE).

columns.name: ROUTE_CATEGORY
columns.description: The category of the route. “LRT” refers to light rail transit, i.e. the CTrain. “BRT” refers to bus rapid transit, i.e. routes designed to transport passengers over long distances quickly, with few stops. “SCHOOL” refers to routes designed to serve students attending school. “REGULAR” refers to typical Calgary Transit bus routes. “SPECIAL” refers to routes providing specialized service that do not fall into the previous categories.

columns.name: ROUTE_LONG_NAME
columns.description: The full name of the route

description: This table relates Calgary Transit stops to Calgary Transit routes. The data source is the General Transit Feed Specification (GTFS) available on the City of Calgary Open Data which is released by Calgary Transit Service Design on at least a quarterly basis. A point will exist multiple times if the stop it used my multiple routes. One point per stop per route.

name: Waste Composition 2019 - Garbage

columns.name: Material Diversion Potential
columns.description: Identifies whether the material is garbage, or could be compostable or recyclable.  Note that compostable material in non-compostable packaging, such as food or animal waste in plastic bags, is considered "Compostable" in this categorization.

columns.name: Proper Disposal Location
columns.description: Identifies where the material should be disposed, according to current diversion programs.  Note that compostable material in non-compostable packaging is considered "Black Cart" in this categorization, because it is correctly disposed in its packaged state.  Potentially Divertible materials are those for which diversion technology exists, but not in The City’s current programs, e.g. expanded polystyrene, compostable plastic utensils and plates, or mattresses. Other Diversion Programs materials are those that can be diverted in other City of Calgary or provincial programs, e.g. electronics, or household hazardous waste.

columns.name: Sector
columns.description: Identifies the sector sampled: Single family residential, Multi-family residential, Industrial Commercial and Institutional (ICI), and Construction and Demolition

columns.name: Material Category
columns.description: Basic categorization of material types

columns.name: Material Subcategory
columns.description: More detailed categorization of material types.

columns.name: Weight %
columns.description: Weight per cent of each material.  Material subcategories may not sum to 100% due to rounding.

description: Waste Composition studies are periodically conducted by Waste and Recycling Services to help assess the performance of diversion and education programs and inform improvements and new program design. In 2019, The City of Calgary assessed the waste composition of Black Carts and garbage bins, in the residential (single family and multi-family) sector and for businesses and organizations. More detailed information about each sector can be found in the description of the filtered datasets. The weig

name: Tree Canopy 2020
description: Tree Canopy contains polygons outlining the extent of tree cover for all land within the City of Calgary. The tree canopy was derived by Remote Sensing software using City of Calgary Orthophotos and Lidar. The resulting data was then quality controlled and edited using various photogrammetric and processing tools.

description: The Regional Transportation Model (RTM) is a computer simulation of the city and surrounding region. This file contains the road network assumptions for the 2015 Scenario from the 2015 CTP/MDP Scenario Series. This series is a time series of future forecasts that are used in transportation planning analysis. The series contains five horizons from 2015 to 2076 and includes roads classified as collector and above and does not include local roads. his data is a representation, not an exact replica,

Methodology The Citizen Satisfaction survey is a telephone survey conducted with a randomly selected sample of Calgarians aged 18 years and older. The survey is conducted on an annual basis. The data are weighted to ensure the overall sample’s quadrant, ward, and age/gende

description: (Note: Updated inundation maps for 1:2 to 1:1000 floods are available from Alberta Environment and Parks (2020). The new draft maps can be viewed here: https://floods.alberta.ca/?app_code=FI&mapType=Draft) These inundation maps show whether a property is at risk for various sized river floods. The size of flood shown on this map has a 1/10 or a 10% chance of occurring in any year. The three distinct types of inundation shown on the maps are: o Inundation - Area flooded overland due to riverbank

name: Calgary Open Budget
description: The City of Calgary creates a four-year budget that is updated annually to reflect the changing needs and priorities of Calgarians. This dataset provides detailed budget information for the 2023 – 2026 Service Plans & Budgets. View the 2026 budget here

name: Traffic Counts at Permanent stations
description: Permanent counts stations are used in trend analysis of traffic patterns around the city. The stations allow us to create seasonal and weekly variations for traffic data so that we can normalize any portable studies that are of short duration.

description: This data was collected as part of The City of Calgary's two-year shared electric scooter and dockless bike share pilots. The trips in this dataset occurred between July 1st, 2019 and September 30th, 2019. Excluded from this dataset are trips that lasted fewer than 30 seconds or 100 metres, and trips where the geospatial data quality made it unsuitable for analysis. Trip start times have been generalized to 1-hour intervals. Trip origins and destinations have been generalized by tiling the city

name: Traffic Incidents Archive 2017

name: Municipal Development Plan Urban Structure
description: Municipal Development Plan Map 1. The geospatial information used to create the Municipal Development Plan (MDP) maps is intended for visualization at a citywide scale. The maps provide both direct and conceptual-level support for many of the policies defined throughout the MDP. The geospatial information is representative of the themes shown at the time of the MDP’s approval and in most cases, has not been updated since. Those amendments that have been made are in accordance with the bylaw amen

name: Calgary Regional Transportation Model RTM Zones

columns.name: Region
columns.description: Indicates if the transportation zone is located within the Calgary city limits.  0 = Zone in Calgary; 1 = zone in surrounding region

description: Transportation Zones (TZ) are the basic geographic unit used to simulate travel behaviour in the Calgary Regional Transportation Model (RTM). They are used to organize the input and output data for a forecast scenario.

name: Waste and Recycling Services Performance Measures

columns.name: Residential Waste Generated through Black, Blue and Green Cart Programs (kilograms per household)
columns.description: This measure represents all residential waste generated though Blue, Black and Green Cart Programs.

columns.name: Citizens who are satisfied with the job The City is doing providing Waste and Recycling services (per cent)
columns.description: This measure is based on results from the Spring Pulse Survey. Responses falling into “very satisfied” and “somewhat satisfied” make up the per cent of responses that are satisfied.

columns.name: Residential Waste Diverted from Landfill Through Blue and Green Cart Programs
columns.description: This measure represents the per cent of residential waste that is diverted from landfill through the Blue Cart and Green Cart programs, out of the total waste generated by single-family households.

columns.name: Waste Collection Interruptions per 10,000 Scheduled Stops
columns.description: This measure captures 311 service requests related to interruptions in Black, Blue and Green Cart waste collection and reports the number of interruptions per 10,000 scheduled collection stops.

columns.name: Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emission Reduction from Landfill Gas Management (tonnes CO2e, thousands)
columns.description: This measure represents the reduction in greenhouse gas emissions due to managing landfill gas at the three active landfill sites.

description: Waste & Recycling Performance Measures are used to assess the performance of The City of Calgary's Waste & Recycling service. Five measures have been approved for the 2023-2026 Service Plans and Budgets. See Waste & Recycling plan and budget for more information.

name: Ward Boundaries for 2021 October 18 General Election
description: Ward boundaries have been updated for the 2021 General Election. These boundaries will not come into effect until Election Day October 18, 2021.

name: Intersection Safety Cameras
description: Intersection Safety Cameras (ISCs) are cameras that detect the travel speed of vehicular traffic. The ISC is also able to detect whether a vehicle fails to stop for the red light phase in a signalized intersection. ISCs are deployed at signalized intersections throughout the city with high collision rates and a history of serious injury or fatal collisions. More information on this is available at: http://www.calgary.ca/cps/Pages/Traffic/Speed-on-green-and-red-light-cameras.aspx

description: Census data is an official count of dwelling units and population within those units. The data is physically collected and may be supplemented with other information such as the periodic age/gender distribution data. This additional data allows for better interpretation of the population statistics. Data is presented by ward boundaries, the electoral areas represented by one councillor.

name: Top 100 Licensed Dog Breeds

columns.name: BREED CODE
columns.description: The breed that the owner believes their pet to look the most like.

description: Dog breed information is reported by dog owner upon licensing. Calgary Animal Services does not verify breed info or require proof of breed when collecting this information. Breed info data collected and housed by Animal Services is therefore unvetted and unconfirmed. Top 100 breeds are based on primary reported breed but do include both purebred and mixed breeds.

description: A sanitary service pipe, also known as a sanitary service line, is the way wastewater leaves each home in Calgary. The sanitary service pipe can be thought of as two sections: (1) From the sanitary main to the property line: This part of the pipe is owned and maintained by The City of Calgary. (2) From the property line to your house: This part of the pipe is owned and maintained by the homeowner. description: The Forward Sortation Area Boundary File depicts the boundaries of forward sortation areas (FSAs) derived from postal codes captured from federal census questionnaires. This is based on census dissemination areas. The file is periodically updated, following the federal census which is currently mandated every 5 years (2011, 2016, 2021...). More information is available at: Forward Sortation Area Boundary File

name: Impervious Surface 2021
description: The Impervious Surface 2021 dataset is a collection of polygon features derived from Calgary Digital Aerial Survey mapping linework which have been determined as impervious. Impervious surfaces are defined as any material which will seal the soil surface, impede water infiltration, collect solar heat or deprive tree or vegetation roots of aeration. Impervious surfaces effect many aspects of our City, including storm run off volumes, pollutant concentrations and drainage. The City must understand

name: Calgary Equity Index Overlay Statistics
description: The Calgary Equity Index Overlay Statistics are derived from Citizen Satisfaction surveys and census demographics. The Calgary Equity Index is a decision-making tool designed to measure equity in Calgary, based on a social determinant of health (SDOH) framework. The SDOH are the range of interacting social and economic conditions that influence people’s health and well-being. This index provides an equity lens to examine the ways in which social and economic conditions are experienced and distri

name: Calgary Bikeways
description: This data set contains the on-street bikeway network within the City of Calgary. A map of bikeways and connection trails is also available in fhe following formats: paper hardcopy, electronic, and the CITYonline interactive map. If you’re interested in data relating to Calgary’s bikeways, The City of Calgary’s Pathways and Bikeways app is available for iPhone, iPod and iPad devices and will provide you with information regarding all City of Calgary bikeways. name: Tree Canopy 2022
description: Tree Canopy contains polygons outlining the extent of tree cover for all land within the City of Calgary. The tree canopy was derived by Remote Sensing software using City of Calgary Orthophotos and Lidar. The resulting data was then quality controlled and edited using various photogrammetric and processing tools. name: Parks Water Bodies
description: This dataset identifies the Natural Water Bodies on lands under Calgary Parks Stewardship. It can be a flowing type of water i.e. STREAM or some type of body of water i.e. NATURAL POND. Types of WATER BODIES are: CREEK – CONSTRUCTION, NATURAL CREEK OR STREAM, NATURAL RIVER, OXBOW, NATURAL LAKE, NATURAL POND, SPRING, and WETLAND.

name: Pets Impound Outcomes

columns.name: OUTCOME
columns.description: ‘Other’ includes: Disposal (when a deceased animal comes to Animal Services Centre, typically hit by a car, and we send the body for cremation). DOA (the term used for “dead on arrival” and typically gets used when an animal is taken to a vet clinic deceased, and there is no indication as to why). In Kennel (Still being kenneled at animal service at the time of this data being collected).

description: Impounded cats and dogs are animals sheltered at the Animal Service Centre because they were running at large in their communities, a violation under the Responsible Pet Ownership Bylaw. A cat or dog running at large creates safety issues for the public and for the animal. For more information please visit: http://www.calgary.ca/CSPS/ABS/Pages/Animal-Services/Impounded-cats-dogs.aspx

name: Official Results - General Election 2013

columns.name: Total Votes
columns.description: Total votes cast for the candidate

columns.name: Ward Stations Reporting
columns.description: Number of ward stations that have reported the results.

columns.name: Total Ward Stations
columns.description:
1.	The number of voting stations listed in the Mayor results is the number of regular and special voting stations in that council ward.

2.	The number of voting stations listed in the Councillor results is the number of regular and special voting stations in that council ward.

3.	The number of voting stations listed in the results for Public School Trustees includes the number of regular and special voting stations for the applicable council wards.

4.	The number of voting stations listed in the results for Separate School Trustees includes the number of regular and special voting stations for the applicable council wards plus any voting stations in districts outside of Calgary governed by the Separate School Board.

columns.name: Leading By
columns.description: The amount the leading candidate is leading by. Only the highest candidate has leading by value. All other candidates will have blank value.

columns.name: Acclaimed

columns.name: Total City Wide Stations

columns.name: City Wide Stations Reporting
columns.description: Number of city wide stations that have reported the results.

columns.name: Office Type
columns.description:
Mayor = 1.
Councillor = 2.
Public School Trustees = 3.
Separate School Trustees = 4.

columns.name: Ward
columns.description: A ward of zero denotes the city wide ward. Regular wards are 1-14. Public and Separate School wards are 1-7 and are in format like 1(1,2).

columns.name: Incumbent
columns.description: The candidate is the incumbent in the position.

columns.name: Percentage of Votes
columns.description: Of the votes cast this shows the percentage cast for the candidate.

name: Traffic Volumes for 2022
description: Average Daily Weekday Traffic (ADWT) is the average number of vehicles in 24 hours adjusted for seasonal variation to represent the average weekday. The number on this map represents two-way totals. Volume on Provincial roads are provided by Alberta Transportation as a Weighted Average Annual Daily Traffic(WAADT). This traffic flow map is intended to be a general representation of traffic volumes across the city. Traffic volumes shown are based on 2022 data and may reflect prevailing conditions

This traffic flow map is intended to be a general representation of traffic volumes across the city. Traffic volumes shown are based on 2023 data and may reflect prevailing conditions

name: Central Business District Cordon Count 2014
description: Every year in May, The City conducts the Central Business District (CBD) cordon count. The cordon count is performed at 31 locations around the CBD over a three week time period, collecting data on how people travel into and out of downtown Calgary. The CBD cordon circles the downtown with the Bow River to the north and the railway tracks to the south and from 11 Street in the west to the Elbow River in the east. For additional information on cordon counts, see Central Business District Cordon C

name: Citizen Satisfaction Survey - Perception of Services
description: Since 1997, The City of Calgary has conducted its annual Citizen Satisfaction Survey to ask Calgarians how satisfied they are with City performance, programs, services, and overall quality of life in Calgary. This file is a summary of the services portion of the Citizen Satisfaction survey, and details how satisfied Calgarians are with each service, how important each service is for them, and whether or not The City should invest more, less, or the same amount in that service.

description: Every year in May, The City conducts the Central Business District (CBD) cordon count. The cordon count is performed at ~ 30 locations around the CBD over a four week time period, collecting data on how people travel into and out of downtown Calgary. The CBD cordon circles the downtown with the Bow River to the north and the railway tracks to the south and from 11 Street in the west to the Elbow River in the east. These data represent the total number of persons entering (inbound) and exiting (o

name: Riparian Management Zones
description: Depicts recommended riparian area management category zones along the major rivers and streams of Calgary. These categories are meant to guide land use planning, park management, and river engineering decisions. The spatial mapping of these categories also complements the Riparian Decision Matrix for River Engineering Projects, with the purpose of ensuring bioengineering practices are applied to the maximum extent feasible within The City of Calgary.

name: Landfill Setbacks
description: These maps provide landfill setback information as defined under the Municipal Government Act (MGA) Subdivision and Development Regulation (SDR) Landfill Setback (Section 13) from a City of Calgary Landfill or Waste Management Facility (WMF). The file contains the landfill setbacks for the following City of Calgary Landfills: East Calgary WMF, Shepard WMF, Spyhill WMF, Blackfoot Landfill, Highfield Landfill, Manchester Landfill, Nose Creek Landfill, Ogden Landfill, and Springbank Landfill. description: To help provide Calgarians with the space needed to walk, run, skateboard and cycle, The City of Calgary has implemented an Adaptive Roadways Program (ARP). Under the program, road lanes are closed to motor vehicle traffic so they can be used by people who are walking, running, cycling, rollerblading and the like. See Adaptive Roadways Program and related lane closures for more information.

name: Residential Parking Zones Polygons
description: Some communities in Calgary require a residential parking permit. The Calgary Parking Authority issues residential parking permits and enforces their restrictions, but the City of Calgary establishes residential parking zones. This data shows the communities which have residential parking zones.

name: Water Annual River Withdrawals
description: Total annual river withdrawals is the total volume of water withdrawn from the Bow and Elbow rivers measured in megalitres (ML).

name: Healthy Rivers Sub Watersheds
tags: pine creek and shepard wetland/western headworks canal
description: The polygons delineate the boundaries of each of the major sub-watersheds within City of Calgary boundaries. These include Bow River Direct, Elbow River, Fish Creek, Nose Creek, Pine Creek and Shepard Wetland/Western Headworks Canal. The sub-watersheds delineate the major drainage areas within The City. name: Bike and Pedestrian Counts Monitoring Locations

Using an Eco-Counter machine, cyclists are detected using loops embedded in the pavement. The data provides the locations. The data for these locations is in the Bike and Pedestrian Counts dataset - https://data.calgary.ca/Transportation-Transit/Bike-and-Pedestrian-Counts/pede-tz7g

description: Every year in May, The City conducts the Central Business District (CBD) cordon count. The cordon count is performed at ~ 30 locations around the CBD over a four week time period, collecting data on how people travel into and out of downtown Calgary. name: Calgary Equity Index Ranges
description: The Calgary Equity Index is a decision-making tool designed to measure equity in Calgary, based on a social determinant of health (SDOH) framework. The SDOH are the range of interacting social and economic conditions that influence people’s health and well-being. This index provides an equity lens to examine the ways in which social and economic conditions are experienced and distributed among populations. It will help the City examine where inequities exist in different areas. Information is av

name: Rivers And Streams - Environmental Reserve Setback Guideline
description: Depicts basic components of The City of Calgary's 2007 Environmental Reserve (ER) Setback Guideline policy, including the base setback based on stream order, and the additional setback modifier based on slope. This can be used as a starting point to scope out riparian-associated ER requirements for planned future developments. It can also be used within existing built areas of the city to highlight past lost ER opportunities.

name: Suburban Residential Growth (SRG) Policy Infrastructure Lines
description: This dataset lists growth-oriented capital infrastructure projects that will support future suburban residential growth, and the policies that will be supported by that infrastructure. It includes required capital infrastructure projects for Water, Sanitary, Storm, Transportation and Emergency Response. Duplicate infrastructure may appear when more than one policy is supported by that infrastructure.

name: Voting Subdivision - 2025
description: The voting subdivision defines the area served by a voting station which is where an elector casts his/her vote. It is an area of a local jurisdiction or ward designated as a voting subdivision by the Returning Officer. The Returning Officer may divide the local jurisdiction into voting subdivisions and may alter their boundaries. Related information: Visit Elections Alberta website

name: City of Calgary-Owned Buildings: Environmental Performance Metrics

columns.name: Total (Location-Based) GHG Emissions Intensity (kgCO2e/m²)
columns.description: The sum of Direct Emissions and Indirect Emissions (location-based) divided by the gross floor area, measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalents. Direct Emissions are emissions associated with onsite fuel combustion (e.g. combustion of natural gas or fuel oil). Indirect (Location-Based) GHG Emissions consumptions a regional emissions factor for electricity, and national factors for district fuels. Onsite Green Power has zero emissions, but Offsite Green Power is treated as regular grid electricity

columns.name: Total (Market-Based) GHG Emissions Intensity (kgCO2e/m²)
columns.description: The sum of Direct Emissions and Indirect Emissions (marked-based) divided by the gross floor area, measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalents. Direct Emissions are emissions associated with onsite fuel combustion (e.g. combustion of natural gas or fuel oil). Indirect (Market-Based) GHG Emissions calculates both Onsite and Offsite Green Power as zero emissions, or a custom emissions factor may be consumption

columns.name: Property GFA - Self-Reported (m²)
columns.description: The property gross floor area in square meters as measured by the owner. The gross floor area is measured from the outside surface of the exterior walls of the building (it is also acceptable to measure from the inside perimeter of the exterior walls if that is more readily available). This includes all areas inside the building(s) including supporting areas. Gross floor area is not the same as rentable space

columns.name: Postal Code
columns.description: The postal code that the property is located in

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city that the property is located in

columns.name: Address 1
columns.description: The civic address of the property

columns.name: Property Name
columns.description: The name of the property

columns.name: Green Power - Onsite and Offsite (kWh)
columns.description: Total annual energy consumed from onsite and offsite renewables, measured in kilowatt-hours. Onsite Green Power is the power generated from an onsite renewable system. Offsite Green Power refers to the total amount of green power sourced from offsite locations, including green power purchased either bundled with grid electricity or as unbundled Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) with a contractual right to claim and retire them, onsite green power involved in REC arbitrage transactions where RECs from onsite generation are sold and substituted with other RECs, and onsite green power exported to the grid where the associated RECs are retained or arbitraged

columns.name: Year Ending
columns.description: The calendar year that the data was measured or modelled

columns.name: Electricity Use – Generated from Onsite Renewable Systems and Used Onsite (kWh)
columns.description: The portion of energy generated by onsite solar or wind systems that was consumed at the property. This excludes energy exported to the grid. If all Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) for this energy are retained, this metric is equivalent to “Green Power – Onsite.

columns.name: Electricity Use – Generated from Onsite Renewable Systems and Exported (kWh)
columns.description: Energy exported to the grid from on-site solar or wind generation. This value is required for calculating actual consumption under net-metered billing. Exported energy is excluded from annual consumption and other performance metrics

columns.name: Percent of Total Electricity Generated from Onsite Renewable Systems
columns.description: The percentage of total electricity consumption supplied by onsite renewable generation (produced and consumptiond onsite), regardless of Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) ownership

columns.name: Percent of Electricity that is Green Power
columns.description: The percent of total annual electricity consumed that is either onsite or offsite green power

columns.name: Electricity - Weather Normalized Site Electricity Use (Grid and Onsite Renewables) (kWh)
columns.description: Total annual energy consumed at the property from the grid and onsite renewables, normalized to typical weather, measured in kilowatt-hours

columns.name: Electricity - Weather Normalized Site Electricity Intensity (Grid and Onsite Renewables) (kWh/m²)
columns.description: Total annual energy consumed at the property from the grid and onsite renewables dvidied by the gross floor area, normalized to typical weather, measured in kilowatt-hours

columns.name: Avoided Emissions - Onsite and Offsite Green Power (Metric Tons CO2e)
columns.description: The combined total of onsite and offsite avoided emissions measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalents. Onsite avoided emissions refer to the emissions reductions achieved by generating renewable energy onsite and retaining the associated Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs), which displace grid-supplied electricity. These reductions are calculated based on the amount of onsite green power consumed and the non-baseload emissions factor for the applicable eGRID subregion. Offsite avoided emissions refer to emissions reductions associated with green power sourced from offsite locations, including purchased green power bundled with grid electricity or as unbundled RECs, onsite green power involved in REC arbitrage transactions, and onsite green power exported to the grid where the RECs are retained or arbitraged

columns.name: Total (Location-Based) GHG Emissions (Metric Tons CO2e)
columns.description: The sum of Direct Emissions and Indirect Emissions (location-based), measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalents. Direct Emissions are emissions associated with onsite fuel combustion (e.g. combustion of natural gas or fuel oil). Indirect (Location-Based) GHG Emissions consumptions a regional emissions factor for electricity, and national factors for district fuels. Onsite Green Power has zero emissions, but Offsite Green Power is treated as regular grid electricity

columns.name: Total (Market-Based) GHG Emissions (Metric Tons CO2e)
columns.description: The sum of Direct Emissions and Indirect Emissions (marked-based), measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalents. Direct Emissions are emissions associated with onsite fuel combustion (e.g. combustion of natural gas or fuel oil). Indirect (Market-Based) GHG Emissions calculates both Onsite and Offsite Green Power as zero emissions, or a custom emissions factor may be consumption

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Score
columns.description: The United States Environmental Protection Agency's 1 - 100 ENERGY STAR score is an external benchmark for assessing the performance of commercial buildings. The ENERGY STAR score, expressed as a number on a simple 1 - 100 scale, rates performance on a percentile basis: buildings with a score of 50 perform better than 50% of their peers; buildings earning a score of 75 or higher are in the top quartile of energy performance

name: Impervious Surface 2023
description: The Impervious Surface 2023 dataset is a collection of polygon features derived from Calgary Digital Aerial Survey mapping linework which have been determined as impervious. Impervious surfaces are defined as any material which will seal the soil surface, impede water infiltration, collect solar heat or deprive tree or vegetation roots of aeration. Impervious surfaces effect many aspects of our city, including storm runoff volumes, pollutant concentrations and drainage. The city must understand

name: Tree Canopy Raw Data 2024
description: Tree Canopy 2024 contains polygons outlining the extent of tree cover for all land within the City of Calgary. It was derived by using custom algorithms and City of Calgary RGBN stereo aerial imagery and lidar captured June-July 2024. The resulting data was quality controlled and is used for computing canopy coverage statistics. Trees and shrubs which are 2 meters in height and taller are captured in this dataset.

description: This dataset provides project‑level information for completed buildings participating in the City of Calgary’s Green Building Priority Stream (GBPS). Each record represents a single building permit for a project that has reached construction completion and includes key building characteristics such as entry pathway, floor area, construction value (where disclosed), and modelled energy, greenhouse gas (GHG), and energy cost performance based on documentation submitted at the building permit stage

name: 2021 Federal Census Population and Dwellings by Community
description: Population and Dwellings Statistics per Community from the 2021 Census of Canada. The data was provided by Statistics Canada, acquired by Community Strategies, and compiled by The City of Calgary. This is a one-time load of Statistics Canada federal census data from 2021 applied to the Communities, Wards, and City geographical boundaries current as of 2022 (so they will likely not match the current year's boundaries). Update frequency is every 5 years.
